mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward 20-day returns because higher rates increase the discount rate applied to future cash flows, depressing equity valuations. This effect operates continuously regardless of the rate level.
evidence_refs: fact:Standard discounted cash flow model predicts inverse relationship between discount rates and asset prices.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns is positive (indicating wrong sign) over any 3-year window, or the absolute correlation is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% from a low base, it signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to downward pressure on equity markets over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical recessions have often been preceded by rising unemployment above 5%.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns in at least 60% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate synchronized weakening in two major components of aggregate demand, foreshadowing a contraction in corporate revenues and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST month-over-month decline and RSAFS month-over-month decline
evidence_refs: fact:Housing and consumption are key drivers of GDP.
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce negative average 60-day forward returns or its information ratio is below 0.3 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment where the consumer price index exceeds 3%, any increase in the federal funds rate further tightens financial conditions and compresses equity valuations, producing negative short-term returns within the next week.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical Fed tightening cycles often precede market declines.
failure_condition: In instances where trigger condition holds, the mean 5-day forward return is not negative and significant (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls signal healthy labor market conditions that support consumer spending and economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Employment growth is a leading indicator of economic strength.
failure_condition: The correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and 60-day forward returns is negative or below 0.1 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising above 1,500 thousand units indicate strong demand in the housing sector, which boosts construction activity and related industries, supporting positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a leading sector in economic cycles.
failure_condition: When HOUST is above 1500, the 20-day forward returns are not positive on average (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is high relative to the unemployment rate (suggesting tight monetary policy in a weak labor market), it signals an increased risk of recession and negative equity returns over short horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:Taylor rule suggests high rates when unemployment is low; here both high signals tension.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields 5-day forward returns that are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the signal is not predictive (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines below a trend threshold (index < 110), it signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate profits and leads to negative stock market returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: When INDPRO < 110, the average 60-day forward return is not negative or the hit rate is below 50% over a 2-year rolling window.
